done_summary:
Completed triage of all 30 support tickets. Output files:

**`/workspace/triage_results.json`** — Structured JSON with per-ticket classification, urgency, and duplicate tracking.

**Key findings:**
- **6 urgent tickets**: 003 (login outage), 014/023/030 (API rate limits cluster), 016 (API 500 errors), 028 (account compromise)
- **4 spam/noise tickets**: 004 (Pixar collectables), 009 (phishing), 021 (affiliate spam), 029 (extortion)
- **2 duplicate clusters**: 
  - `["007", "011"]` — Dashboard sharing permissions (same account MC-3309, two sides of same issue)
  - `["014", "023", "030"]` — API rate limits (three follow-ups from same sender)
- **Category distribution**: 7 bug-reports, 4 billing-refunds, 3 feature-requests, 2 incident-active, 2 account-management, 2 auth-permissions, 2 general-questions, 2 external-business, 1 security-incident, 1 legal-compliance, 4 spam-or-noise

**`/workspace/README.md`** — Summary with recommended processing order and ambiguous call highlights.

**`/workspace/decisions/`** — 5 ADRs documenting non-obvious classification decisions (rate limits, extortion email, API 500s, downtime refund, duplicate detection methodology).
